title: Scava invites entries for Annual Visual Expressions
description: GEORGE NEWS - The South Cape Association of...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The South Cape Association of Visual Arts (Scava) invites local artists to enter for its Annual Visual Expressions competition and exhibition 2024.


> It is open to members of Scava as well as non-members. The submission of entries will take place on Friday 30 August between 09:00 and 16:00 and on Saturday 31 August between 09:00 and 12:00.


The exhibition will then run till 1 October in the George Museum Sayer's Auditorium.

The winners for the various categories will be announced at an awards ceremony on Friday 6 September (17:30 for 18:00). Artists and art lovers may RSVP for the gala and prize-giving event by Saturday 31 August to [email protected].

Odin to benefit

For this year’s Visual Expressions exhibition, a special project for Scava members is being introduced where all participating artists are invited to create a painting on an unframed deep-stretched canvas (thick box profile of about 3,5cm), measuring 15 x 15cm.


> These artworks will be displayed together as a collective whole in the foyer of the auditorium and will be sold for R500 each. Half of the proceeds will go to the artist, and the other half will be donated to Oudtshoorn Dogs in Need (Odin).


Artists are encouraged to create multiple pieces but must submit at least one. Non-Scava members participating in the competition are also welcome to join this initiative.

These artworks will not form part of the competition and will not be judged.

Scava chairperson René Scribante (second from left) with three of the 2023 Visual Expressions category winners, from left: Michelle Henn, Helen Burton and Heinrich Botha. Photo: Alida de Beer

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
